compiled_state.title: As a DSO practitioner, the Deep Sonnet C reviewer applies deep hygiene, design, and maintainability checks

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Enhance the Deep Sonnet C hygiene specialist (code-reviewer-deep-hygiene.md) with researched sub-criteria for code hygiene, design quality, and maintainability — tuned to this project's architecture patterns.

**Why**: The hygiene specialist catches design and maintainability issues in high-risk changes. Its generic checklist doesn't account for this project's architecture (hook dispatcher pattern, skill file conventions, plugin portability requirements, config-driven paths).

**Scope**:
- IN: Updating hygiene/design/maintainability checklist items with project-aware patterns; adding sub-criteria for plugin architecture compliance; researching maintainability best practices for bash/Python polyglot projects
- OUT: Modifying correctness or verification dimensions; changing the base reviewer contract

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the Deep Hygiene reviewer includes sub-criteria for project architecture compliance (hook dispatcher pattern, skill file structure, config-driven paths via dso-config.conf)
  ← Satisfies: 'Review Intelligence & Precision'
- When this story is complete, maintainability sub-criteria flag violations of plugin portability (hardcoded paths, host-project assumptions not mediated by config)
  ← Satisfies: 'Review Intelligence & Precision'
- When this story is complete, unit tests verify the updated agent file structure
  ← Satisfies: 'Review Intelligence & Precision'

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Project-specific patterns should be expressed as general principles where possible to maintain portability
- [Testing] Validate against recent hygiene-flagged reviews to check false positive rate
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 3/6: Tests written ✓ — 6 RED tests confirm missing sub-criteria (T4: hook dispatcher, T5: skill file structure, T6: config-driven paths, T8: host-project mediation, T9: bash hygiene patterns, T12: generated agent sync)
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 4/6: Implementation complete ✓ — delta updated with hook dispatcher pattern, skill file structure, config-driven paths, plugin portability, bash/python hygiene sub-criteria; build-review-agents.sh regenerated agent file
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 5/6: Validation passed ✓ — all 17 new tests GREEN, test-reviewer-dimension-names.sh 6/6 PASS, no regressions in light/standard checklist tests
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 6/6: Done ✓ — self-check passed: no correctness/verification scope bleed in delta, all new sub-criteria mapped to hygiene/design/maintainability dimensions only, generated agent file verified
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Fixed: Enhanced Deep hygiene reviewer with architecture compliance, portability checks, bash/Python hygiene patterns
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
